danger_signals.visual_form: Cartoon police force characters in game world
danger_signals.meaning: Law enforcement threat; player risks arrest if surrounded
Escalating danger state; more violations increase arrest likelihood and bail amounts
Escalation from generic police to direct confrontation; player must pay bail or receive lecture

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items scattered through game world
safety_signals.meaning: Resource accumulation for bail payment; mitigates arrest consequences
Protected financial state; allows players to secure bail money against arrest
Neutral zone for arranging favorable social outcomes (date with Holli Would)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Police uniform/authority insignia (inferred from 'cartoon police force' description)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Generic police officers vs. Detective Frank Harris (named authority figure)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Patrol areas throughout game world; presence indicates law enforcement jurisdiction

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player persona shifts from law-abiding to wanted based on violations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Restricted access zones (malt shop entry requires candy collection); bail system enforces class distinction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Free movement; Popper Police encounters avoidable
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Police maintain patrol patterns; player can navigate safely with evasion

world_state_to_visual.world_state: High law-breaking count + insufficient bail money
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Detective Frank Harris summoned; arrest sequence triggered
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Forced dialogue/lecture; player tossed back to ground level or forced payment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Access to Holli's apartment building exterior becomes critical progression path
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vertical navigation challenge; failure results in ejection back to ground level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Malt shop entrance restricted; pawn shop transactions gated by nickel possession
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must farm Las Vegas location for currency before accessing candy/restricted areas

environmental_storytelling.example: Las Vegas serves as economic hub for coin farming; Cool World bank provides safe storage; Slash Club mediates social advancement; pawn shop enables economic trades—all reinforce capitalist/urban commerce narrative parallel to movie source
Malt shop entry requires candy collection; Holli's apartment requires successful date arrangement—restricted spaces communicate progression requirements and social hierarchy
Holli Would represents the goal/temptation; Detective Frank Harris embodies law enforcement consequence; Jack Deebs' avoidance of both creates central tension

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Maintain predictable patrol patterns; player can avoid via evasion and positioning
Deliver lecture or demand bail payment; escalation from generic police to named authority
Can move freely; police avoidable through spatial awareness
Forced arrest; dialogue sequence; currency penalty; ground-level reset

notes: Cool World SNES version diverges from other platform ports by functioning as adventure game with Jack Deebs as protagonist avoiding both law enforcement and romantic entanglement. Visual communication emphasizes economic gatekeeping (currency collection), spatial restriction (location access), and law enforcement threat escalation (generic police → Detective Harris). World state manifests through dual consequence systems: legal (arrest/bail) and romantic (date arrangement requiring social hub access). The game communicates societal structure through capitalist transactions (nickels, bail, pawn shop trades) and authority presence (Popper Police patrols). Limited source material prevents detailed color/symbol extraction; design relies on familiar urban/game-world archetypes rather than distinctive faction visual identity.
